description:
You are a compliance officer who has been asked to verify the exact version of a legacy
  command-line program that is still present on several production hosts.  
  In this particular host, the binary has already been staged for you at

      /home/user/legacy/bin/old_tool

  Your audit requirements are:

  1. Run the tool once, requesting its version (use the classic GNU-style “--version” flag
     that the tool still understands).  
  2. Capture the version the tool prints out and write it to a *new* log file located at

      /home/user/audit_logs/old_tool_version_YYYYMMDD.log

     where YYYYMMDD is today’s calendar date according to the system clock
     (e.g. 20240625 for 25 June 2024).  
     • If the directory /home/user/audit_logs does not yet exist, create it first.  
  3. The log file **must contain exactly three lines, in the precise order below**.
     The automated grader will do a strict, character-for-character comparison:

         DATE=<yyyy-mm-dd>
         TOOL_COMMAND=/home/user/legacy/bin/old_tool --version
         TOOL_VERSION=old_tool version v0.9.8-legacy

     • DATE must be today’s date in ISO format (the same value you would get from the
       command “date +%F”).  
     • TOOL_COMMAND must be written verbatim, including the full absolute path and the
       “--version” flag.  
     • TOOL_VERSION must reproduce exactly the single line of output the tool prints.

  That is all.  Only one invocation of the legacy tool is necessary; the task is finished
  once the correctly-formatted log file is present.  No additional commentary or files
  should be produced.
truth:
Pre-existing artefacts (already present before the agent begins):

    1. Directory:  /home/user/legacy/bin          (permissions 755)
    2. File:       /home/user/legacy/bin/old_tool (permissions 755)
       Contents:
         #!/usr/bin/env bash
         echo "old_tool version v0.9.8-legacy"

  Expected artefacts **after** the agent has completed the task:

    1. Directory: /home/user/audit_logs           (permissions 755, can be newly created)
    2. File:      /home/user/audit_logs/old_tool_version_YYYYMMDD.log
       • YYYYMMDD must match the date on which the test is executed.
       • File content – exactly three lines with no trailing whitespace:

         DATE=<yyyy-mm-dd>
         TOOL_COMMAND=/home/user/legacy/bin/old_tool --version
         TOOL_VERSION=old_tool version v0.9.8-legacy

       where <yyyy-mm-dd> is the same date component represented in ISO form
       (identical to the output of “date +%F” inside the container at runtime).
